The outbreak has spread to new provinces including Tshopo and Haut-Uele, and the first case outside Africa has been confirmed in France. Response efforts are hampered by funding shortages, unpaid health workers, and the closure of USAID, while new diagnostic tests and treatment trials offer hope.

incidents.canonical_name: International food safety event: Infant formula and products containing arachidonic acid oil contaminated with cereulide toxin - Multi-country
incidents.summary: Nestlé recalled several batches of infant formula products (including SMA, BEBA, and NAN) across Europe after detecting cereulide toxin. The EU food safety regulator (EFSA) subsequently set a maximum safe limit for cereulide in infant formula, and France lowered its permitted threshold in response to the recalls. ECDC and EFSA also published a Rapid Outbreak Assessment confirming that continued recalls reduce exposure risk.
